news_items.headline: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5, its first fully retrained base model since GPT-4.5
news_items.summary:
OpenAI launches GPT-5.5, its first fully retrained base model since GPT-4.5

The model, codenamed “Spud,” is designed to complete complex multi-step tasks with minimal human direction. It sets new benchmarks in agentic coding, computer use, and knowledge work, while matching GPT-5.4’s per-token latency. API access is delayed pending additional safety work.

# LLM Leaderboard: Best AI Models Ranked (April 2026) - DEV Community

Published: 2026-04-23T07:33:09+00:00

## Summary

There is no single best AI model in the world in April 2026, with Claude Opus 4.7 dominating coding benchmarks at 82% on SWE-bench Verified and ranking first on LM Arena with 1504 Elo rating. Three models tied at the top of the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (score of 57), including Claude 4. 7, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview, and GPT-5.4. IFEval Benchmark 2026: 15 LLM scores | BenchLM.ai

# Instruction-Following Eval (IFEval)

A benchmark that evaluates language models' ability to follow verifiable instructions such as formatting constraints, keyword inclusion/exclusion, length limits, and structural requirements.

## Top models on IFEval — April 22, 2026

As of April 22, 2026, Qwen3.5-27B leads the IFEval leaderboard with 95% , followed by Qwen3.6 Plus(94.3%) and Kimi K2.5(93.9%).

Author: Asif Razzaq

## Summary

Alibaba's Qwen Team has released Qwen3.6-27B, the first dense open-weight model in the Qwen 3.6 family, a 27-billion-parameter model available for coding agents. Published: 2026-04-22T09:52:01+00:00
Author: James Dargan

## Summary

Loop, a full-stack AI platform for logistics and supply chains, has raised $95 million in Series C funding to expand its platform across broader enterprise use cases. The funding was led by Valor Equity Partners, with participation from Founders Fund, Index Ventures, J.P. Morgan Growth Equity Partners and others. news_items.headline: White House Framework Calls for Federal Preemption of State AI Laws
news_items.summary:
# White House Framework Calls for Federal Preemption of State AI Laws | Cranfill Sumner LLP - JDSupra

Published: 2026-04-21T21:08:38+00:00

## Summary

The White House has released a national legislative framework urging Congress to preempt state-level artificial intelligence (AI) laws that it believes are overly burdensome and inconsistent. The framework suggests a centralization of AI regulation at the federal level, following Executive Order 14365, entitled Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence, issued on December 11, 2025. news_items.headline: White House Unveils National AI Legislative Framework to Guide Federal Policy
news_items.summary:
# White House Unveils National AI Legislative Framework to Guide Federal Policy - CDO Magazine

Published: 2026-04-21T17:18:02+00:00

## Summary

The White House has released a national legislative framework aimed at shaping federal AI policy and guiding Congress towards comprehensive regulation of the technology. The framework includes key priorities such as child safety, intellectual property protections, free speech, innovation, and national security. It also urges Congress to avoid overly burdensome rules that could hinder innovation, while addressing risks such as harmful content, misuse
